
```{r} 
#EDA and preprocessing
#upload relevant packages
#install.packages(c("readxl", "dplyr", "janitor")) #install packages if needed
library(readxl)
library(dplyr)
library(janitor)
library(INLA)

#EDA and data pre-processing
#Let's start by defining the variables to keep
vars_keep <- c(
  "STOP_ID",
  "SUSPECT_ARRESTED_FLAG",
  "SUSPECT_REPORTED_AGE",
  "SUSPECT_SEX",
  "SUSPECT_RACE_DESCRIPTION",
  "SUSPECT_HEIGHT",
  "SUSPECT_WEIGHT",
  "STOP_WAS_INITIATED",
  "SUSPECTED_CRIME_DESCRIPTION",
  "OBSERVED_DURATION_MINUTES",
  "STOP_DURATION_MINUTES",
  "WEAPON_FOUND_FLAG",
  "FRISKED_FLAG",
  "SEARCHED_FLAG",
  "STOP_FRISK_DATE",
  "STOP_FRISK_TIME",
  "STOP_LOCATION_BORO_NAME",
  "ISSUING_OFFICER_COMMAND_CODE"
)

#We also keep the variable STOP_ID to check for duplicates

#load the data
sqf_raw <- read_excel("sqf-2025.xlsx")

#select the relevant variables
sqf <- sqf_raw[, vars_keep]
head(sqf)

#Check the data
dim(sqf)
names(sqf)
glimpse(sqf)
summary(sqf)

#Store the name of numeric and categorical variables
id_vars <- c("STOP_ID")

categorical_vars <- c(
  "SUSPECT_ARRESTED_FLAG",
  "SUSPECT_SEX",
  "SUSPECT_RACE_DESCRIPTION",
  "STOP_WAS_INITIATED",
  "SUSPECTED_CRIME_DESCRIPTION",
  "WEAPON_FOUND_FLAG",
  "FRISKED_FLAG",
  "SEARCHED_FLAG",
  "STOP_LOCATION_BORO_NAME",
  "ISSUING_OFFICER_COMMAND_CODE"
)

numeric_vars <- c(
  "SUSPECT_REPORTED_AGE",
  "SUSPECT_HEIGHT",
  "SUSPECT_WEIGHT",
  "OBSERVED_DURATION_MINUTES",
  "STOP_DURATION_MINUTES"
)

date_time_vars <- c(
  "STOP_FRISK_DATE",
  "STOP_FRISK_TIME"
)

#Check for duplicates
sum(duplicated(sqf$STOP_ID))

#No duplicated rows, we can proceed with the analysis

sqf$STOP_ID <- NULL #remove the STOP_ID variable as it is not useful for the analysis

head(sqf)

for (v in categorical_vars) {
  cat("Variable:", v, "\n")
  
  counts <- table(sqf[[v]])
  perc <- round(prop.table(counts) * 100, 2)
  
  freq_table <- data.frame(
    Level = names(counts),
    Count = as.vector(counts),
    Percentage = as.vector(perc)
  )
  
  print(freq_table)
}

#Missing values are encoded as (null). At this stage, we remove all rows containing at least one (null) entry in the selected variables.

#convert (null) to NA
sqf[sqf == "(null)"] <- NA

#Let's check how many rows we lose by removing all rows with at least one NA

cat("Number of rows lost by removing rows with at least one NA:", nrow(sqf) - nrow(na.omit(sqf)))

#Let's proceed by deleting NAs

sqf_clean <- na.omit(sqf)

#Lets tranform into factors the categorical variables with small cardinality
factor_vars_small <- c(
  "SUSPECT_SEX",
  "STOP_WAS_INITIATED",
  "STOP_LOCATION_BORO_NAME",
  "SUSPECT_ARRESTED_FLAG",
  "WEAPON_FOUND_FLAG",
  "FRISKED_FLAG",
  "SEARCHED_FLAG",
  "SUSPECT_RACE_DESCRIPTION")

sqf_clean[factor_vars_small] <- lapply(sqf_clean[factor_vars_small], factor)

#Let's encode ISSUING_OFFICER_COMMAND_CODE as a factor as well, even if it has high cardinality,
#because it represents a police command code,  and it will be used later
#as a grouping factor in a hierarchical model.

sqf_clean$ISSUING_OFFICER_COMMAND_CODE <- factor(sqf_clean$ISSUING_OFFICER_COMMAND_CODE)

#Lets check the proportion of each level within categorical variables with high cardinality only
factor_vars_high <- "SUSPECTED_CRIME_DESCRIPTION"
factor_vars_high
for (v in factor_vars_high) {
 
  cat("Variable:", v, "\n")
  
  counts <- table(sqf_clean[[v]])
  perc <- round(prop.table(counts) * 100, 2)
  
  freq_table <- data.frame(
    Level = names(counts),
    Count = as.vector(counts),
    Percentage = as.vector(perc)
  )
  
  print(freq_table)
}

#Recode rare crime categories into OTHER if their frequency is below 0.1%
crime_counts <- table(sqf_clean$SUSPECTED_CRIME_DESCRIPTION)
crime_perc <- prop.table(crime_counts) * 100

rare_crimes <- names(crime_perc[crime_perc < 0.1])
rare_crimes

#group rare crimes into "OTHER" category
sqf_clean$SUSPECTED_CRIME_DESCRIPTION[sqf_clean$SUSPECTED_CRIME_DESCRIPTION %in% rare_crimes] <- "OTHER"

sqf_clean$SUSPECTED_CRIME_DESCRIPTION <- factor(sqf_clean$SUSPECTED_CRIME_DESCRIPTION)
dim(sqf_clean)

#Now let's check the distribution of numeric features to spot potential outliers

#Check that numeric_vars actually contain numeric variables
sapply(sqf_clean[numeric_vars], class)
#Convert to numeric

sqf_clean$SUSPECT_HEIGHT <- as.numeric(sqf_clean$SUSPECT_HEIGHT)
sqf_clean$SUSPECT_WEIGHT <- as.numeric(sqf_clean$SUSPECT_WEIGHT)

#Check number of na in SUSPECT_HEIGHT
sum(is.na(as.numeric(sqf_clean$SUSPECT_HEIGHT)))
sum(is.na(as.numeric(sqf_clean$SUSPECT_WEIGHT)))

summary(sqf_clean[numeric_vars])

#Let's plot a boxplot for numeric variables to check for outliers
par(mfrow = c(2, 3))

for (v in numeric_vars) {
  boxplot(sqf_clean[[v]], main = v, horizontal = TRUE)
}

#plot histograms
par(mfrow = c(2, 3))
for (v in numeric_vars) {
  hist(sqf_clean[[v]], main = v, xlab = v)
}

#Correlation matrix for numeric variables
cor_matrix <- cor(sqf_clean[numeric_vars], use = "complete.obs")
print(cor_matrix)


#Check distribution of log(time variables)
sqf_clean$log_OBSERVED_DURATION <- log1p(sqf_clean$OBSERVED_DURATION_MINUTES)
sqf_clean$log_STOP_DURATION <- log1p(sqf_clean$STOP_DURATION_MINUTES)

par(mfrow = c(1, 2))
hist(sqf_clean$log_OBSERVED_DURATION, main = "log(1 + OBSERVED_DURATION_MINUTES)", xlab = "log_OBSERVED_DURATION")
hist(sqf_clean$log_STOP_DURATION, main = "log(1 + STOP_DURATION_MINUTES)", xlab = "log_STOP_DURATION")
```

#---------FIT MODEL 1 in inla------------#
```{r} 
#Keep only variables used in Model 1
model1_data <- sqf_clean[, c(
  "SUSPECT_ARRESTED_FLAG",
  "SUSPECT_REPORTED_AGE",
  "log_STOP_DURATION",
  "SUSPECT_SEX",
  "SUSPECT_RACE_DESCRIPTION",
  "STOP_WAS_INITIATED",
  "SUSPECTED_CRIME_DESCRIPTION",
  "FRISKED_FLAG",
  "SEARCHED_FLAG",
  "WEAPON_FOUND_FLAG",
  "STOP_LOCATION_BORO_NAME"
)]

#define a binary response for the binomial model
model1_data$SUSPECT_ARRESTED_FLAG <- ifelse(
  model1_data$SUSPECT_ARRESTED_FLAG == "Y", 1, 0
)

#Centre numeric covariates used in the model
model1_data$SUSPECT_REPORTED_AGE<- model1_data$SUSPECT_REPORTED_AGE -
  mean(model1_data$SUSPECT_REPORTED_AGE)

model1_data$log_STOP_DURATION<- model1_data$log_STOP_DURATION -
  mean(model1_data$log_STOP_DURATION)

#choose reference categories 
model1_data$SUSPECT_SEX <- relevel(model1_data$SUSPECT_SEX, ref = "MALE")
model1_data$SUSPECT_RACE_DESCRIPTION <- relevel(model1_data$SUSPECT_RACE_DESCRIPTION, ref = "BLACK")
model1_data$STOP_WAS_INITIATED <- relevel(model1_data$STOP_WAS_INITIATED, ref = "Based on Self Initiated")
model1_data$SUSPECTED_CRIME_DESCRIPTION <- relevel(model1_data$SUSPECTED_CRIME_DESCRIPTION, ref = "CPW")
model1_data$FRISKED_FLAG <- relevel(model1_data$FRISKED_FLAG, ref = "N")
model1_data$SEARCHED_FLAG <- relevel(model1_data$SEARCHED_FLAG, ref = "N")
model1_data$WEAPON_FOUND_FLAG <- relevel(model1_data$WEAPON_FOUND_FLAG, ref = "N")
model1_data$STOP_LOCATION_BORO_NAME <- relevel(model1_data$STOP_LOCATION_BORO_NAME, ref = "BRONX")

#Priors
prior.fixed <- list(
  mean.intercept = 0,
  prec.intercept = 0.01,   # variace = 100
  mean = 0,
  prec = 0.04              # variance = 25
)

#Fit Model 1
m1 <- inla(
  SUSPECT_ARRESTED_FLAG ~
    SUSPECT_REPORTED_AGE +
    log_STOP_DURATION +
    SUSPECT_SEX +
    SUSPECT_RACE_DESCRIPTION +
    STOP_WAS_INITIATED +
    SUSPECTED_CRIME_DESCRIPTION +
    FRISKED_FLAG +
    SEARCHED_FLAG +
    WEAPON_FOUND_FLAG +
    STOP_LOCATION_BORO_NAME,
  family = "binomial",
  data = model1_data,
  control.fixed = prior.fixed,
  control.compute = list(dic = TRUE, waic = TRUE, cpo = TRUE)
)

#output
summary(m1)

#Fixed effects summary
m1$summary.fixed

#DIC and WAIC
m1$dic$dic
m1$waic$waic

#Marginal log-likelihood
m1$mlik

#Fixed effects summary
m1$summary.fixed

#DIC and WAIC
m1$dic$dic
m1$waic$waic

#Marginal log-likelihood
m1$mlik

#NLSCPO
nlscpo.m1 <- -sum(log(m1$cpo$cpo), na.rm = TRUE)
nlscpo.m1

coef_table <- data.frame(
  Coefficient = rownames(m1$summary.fixed),
  Mean = m1$summary.fixed$mean,
  Lower95 = m1$summary.fixed$`0.025quant`,
  Upper95 = m1$summary.fixed$`0.975quant`,
  AbsMean = abs(m1$summary.fixed$mean),
  row.names = NULL
)

coef_table <- coef_table[order(coef_table$AbsMean, decreasing = TRUE), ]


print(coef_table[1:6, c("Coefficient", "Mean", "Lower95", "Upper95")], row.names = FALSE)
```
#-----Model2-----#
```{r} 
#we start from sqf_clean and build a new dataset for model 2
model2_data <- sqf_clean[, c(
  "SUSPECT_ARRESTED_FLAG",
  "SUSPECT_REPORTED_AGE",
  "SUSPECT_HEIGHT",
  "SUSPECT_WEIGHT",
  "log_OBSERVED_DURATION",
  "log_STOP_DURATION",
  "SUSPECT_SEX",
  "SUSPECT_RACE_DESCRIPTION",
  "STOP_WAS_INITIATED",
  "SUSPECTED_CRIME_DESCRIPTION",
  "FRISKED_FLAG",
  "SEARCHED_FLAG",
  "WEAPON_FOUND_FLAG",
  "STOP_LOCATION_BORO_NAME",
  "STOP_FRISK_DATE",
  "STOP_FRISK_TIME"
)]

#Encode the response variable

model2_data$SUSPECT_ARRESTED_FLAG <- ifelse(
  model2_data$SUSPECT_ARRESTED_FLAG == "Y", 1, 0
)

#Center continuous variables, as in model 1
model2_data$SUSPECT_REPORTED_AGE <- model2_data$SUSPECT_REPORTED_AGE -
  mean(model2_data$SUSPECT_REPORTED_AGE)

model2_data$SUSPECT_HEIGHT <- model2_data$SUSPECT_HEIGHT -
  mean(model2_data$SUSPECT_HEIGHT)

model2_data$SUSPECT_WEIGHT <- model2_data$SUSPECT_WEIGHT -
  mean(model2_data$SUSPECT_WEIGHT)

model2_data$log_OBSERVED_DURATION <- model2_data$log_OBSERVED_DURATION -
  mean(model2_data$log_OBSERVED_DURATION)

model2_data$log_STOP_DURATION <- model2_data$log_STOP_DURATION -
  mean(model2_data$log_STOP_DURATION)

#set reference categories, as in model 1
model2_data$SUSPECT_SEX <- relevel(model2_data$SUSPECT_SEX, ref = "MALE")
model2_data$SUSPECT_RACE_DESCRIPTION <- relevel(model2_data$SUSPECT_RACE_DESCRIPTION, ref = "BLACK")
model2_data$STOP_WAS_INITIATED <- relevel(model2_data$STOP_WAS_INITIATED, ref = "Based on Self Initiated")
model2_data$SUSPECTED_CRIME_DESCRIPTION <- relevel(model2_data$SUSPECTED_CRIME_DESCRIPTION, ref = "CPW")
model2_data$FRISKED_FLAG <- relevel(model2_data$FRISKED_FLAG, ref = "N")
model2_data$SEARCHED_FLAG <- relevel(model2_data$SEARCHED_FLAG, ref = "N")
model2_data$WEAPON_FOUND_FLAG <- relevel(model2_data$WEAPON_FOUND_FLAG, ref = "N")
model2_data$STOP_LOCATION_BORO_NAME <- relevel(model2_data$STOP_LOCATION_BORO_NAME, ref = "BRONX")

#---Temporal variables--#
#Calendar date effect:
#convert STOP_FRISK_DATE to Date and then create an ordered index
model2_data$STOP_FRISK_DATE <- as.Date(model2_data$STOP_FRISK_DATE)

#ordered unique dates
date_levels <- sort(unique(model2_data$STOP_FRISK_DATE))

#integer index for RW1
model2_data$date_index <- match(model2_data$STOP_FRISK_DATE, date_levels)

#check
length(date_levels)

#Time-of-day effect:
#convert HH:MM:SS to seconds from midnight, then we discretize
time_chr <- as.character(model2_data$STOP_FRISK_TIME)
time_posix <- strptime(time_chr, format = "%H:%M:%S")

seconds_from_midnight <- time_posix$hour * 3600 +
  time_posix$min * 60 +
  time_posix$sec

#choose a discretization for the cyclic effect
#30-minute bins -> 48 points over the 24-hour cycle
model2_data$time_bin <- floor(seconds_from_midnight / (30 * 60)) + 1

#safety check: should go from 1 to 48
range(model2_data$time_bin)


#Borough index for random effect

#INLA random effects work comfortably with integer indices
model2_data$boro_id <- as.integer(model2_data$STOP_LOCATION_BORO_NAME)


#Same priors for fixed effects as in model 1
prior.fixed <- list(
  mean.intercept = 0,
  prec.intercept = 0.01,   # variance = 100
  mean = 0,
  prec = 0.04              # variance = 25
)

#PC priors for latent random effects:
#P(sd > 1) = 0.01
pc_prec <- list(
  prec = list(
    prior = "pc.prec",
    param = c(1, 0.01)
  )
)


#Fit Model 2
m2 <- inla(
  SUSPECT_ARRESTED_FLAG ~
    SUSPECT_REPORTED_AGE +
    SUSPECT_HEIGHT +
    SUSPECT_WEIGHT +
    log_OBSERVED_DURATION +
    log_STOP_DURATION +
    SUSPECT_SEX +
    SUSPECT_RACE_DESCRIPTION +
    STOP_WAS_INITIATED +
    SUSPECTED_CRIME_DESCRIPTION +
    FRISKED_FLAG +
    SEARCHED_FLAG +
    WEAPON_FOUND_FLAG +
    f(date_index, model = "rw1",
      hyper = pc_prec) +
    f(time_bin, model = "rw1", cyclic = TRUE,
      hyper = pc_prec) +
    f(boro_id, model = "iid",
      hyper = pc_prec),
  family = "binomial",
  data = model2_data,
  control.fixed = prior.fixed,
  control.compute = list(dic = TRUE, waic = TRUE, cpo = TRUE),
  control.predictor = list(compute = TRUE)
)


#Output

summary(m2)

#Fixed effects
m2$summary.fixed

#Random effects / latent components
m2$summary.hyperpar

#Model comparison criteria
m2$dic$dic
m2$waic$waic
m2$mlik

#CPO-based summary
nlscpo.m2 <- -sum(log(m2$cpo$cpo), na.rm = TRUE)
nlscpo.m2

#Fixed effects summary for Model 2: largest coefficients in absolute value
coef_table_m2 <- data.frame(
  Coefficient = rownames(m2$summary.fixed),
  Mean = m2$summary.fixed$mean,
  Lower95 = m2$summary.fixed$`0.025quant`,
  Upper95 = m2$summary.fixed$`0.975quant`,
  AbsMean = abs(m2$summary.fixed$mean),
  row.names = NULL
)

coef_table_m2 <- coef_table_m2[order(coef_table_m2$AbsMean, decreasing = TRUE), ]

print(coef_table_m2[1:6, c("Coefficient", "Mean", "Lower95", "Upper95")], row.names = FALSE)

#----Model 2 specific plots----#
#Extract posterior summary of borough random effects
boro_re <- m2$summary.random$boro_id

#Recover borough labels using the ID returned by INLA
boro_labels <- levels(model2_data$STOP_LOCATION_BORO_NAME)[boro_re$ID]

# Build plotting data frame on log-odds scale
boro_plot <- data.frame(
  borough = boro_labels,
  mean = boro_re$mean,
  low = boro_re$`0.025quant`,
  high = boro_re$`0.975quant`
)

#Extract time-of-day random effect
time_re <- m2$summary.random$time_bin

# Midpoints of 30-minute bins in hours
time_mid_hours <- seq(0.25, 23.75, by = 0.5)


png("model2_borough_time_effects_logodds.png", width = 1600, height = 800, res = 150)

par(mfrow = c(1, 2), mar = c(8, 5, 4, 2) + 0.1)

#Plot borough random effects on log-odds scale
plot(
  x = 1:nrow(boro_plot),
  y = boro_plot$mean,
  ylim = range(c(boro_plot$low, boro_plot$high)),
  xlim = c(0.7, nrow(boro_plot) + 0.3),
  pch = 16,
  xaxt = "n",
  xlab = "",
  ylab = "Borough effect (log-odds scale)",
  main = "Borough random effects"
)

arrows(
  x0 = 1:nrow(boro_plot),
  y0 = boro_plot$low,
  x1 = 1:nrow(boro_plot),
  y1 = boro_plot$high,
  angle = 90,
  code = 3,
  length = 0.05
)

abline(h = 0, col = "red", lty = 2)

axis(
  1,
  at = 1:nrow(boro_plot),
  labels = boro_plot$borough,
  las = 2,
  cex.axis = 0.9
)

#plot cyclic RW1 effect for time of day on log-odds scale
plot(
  x = time_mid_hours,
  y = time_re$mean,
  type = "l",
  lwd = 2,
  ylim = range(c(time_re$`0.025quant`, time_re$`0.975quant`)),
  xaxt = "n",
  xlab = "Time of day",
  ylab = "Time-of-day effect (log-odds scale)",
  main = "Estimated cyclic RW1 effect for time of day"
)

lines(time_mid_hours, time_re$`0.025quant`, lty = 2)
lines(time_mid_hours, time_re$`0.975quant`, lty = 2)

abline(h = 0, col = "red", lty = 2)

axis(
  1,
  at = seq(0, 22, by = 2),
  labels = sprintf("%02d:00", seq(0, 22, by = 2)),
  las = 2,
  cex.axis = 0.8
)

dev.off()
```
```{r} 
#Model 3: hierarchical extension

#Build dataset for model 3
model3_data <- sqf_clean[, c(
  "SUSPECT_ARRESTED_FLAG",
  "SUSPECT_REPORTED_AGE",
  "SUSPECT_HEIGHT",
  "SUSPECT_WEIGHT",
  "log_OBSERVED_DURATION",
  "log_STOP_DURATION",
  "SUSPECT_SEX",
  "SUSPECT_RACE_DESCRIPTION",
  "STOP_WAS_INITIATED",
  "SUSPECTED_CRIME_DESCRIPTION",
  "FRISKED_FLAG",
  "SEARCHED_FLAG",
  "WEAPON_FOUND_FLAG",
  "STOP_LOCATION_BORO_NAME",
  "STOP_FRISK_DATE",
  "STOP_FRISK_TIME",
  "ISSUING_OFFICER_COMMAND_CODE"
)]

#Encode response variable
model3_data$SUSPECT_ARRESTED_FLAG <- ifelse(
  model3_data$SUSPECT_ARRESTED_FLAG == "Y", 1, 0
)


#Center continuous covariates

model3_data$SUSPECT_REPORTED_AGE <- model3_data$SUSPECT_REPORTED_AGE -
  mean(model3_data$SUSPECT_REPORTED_AGE)

model3_data$SUSPECT_HEIGHT <- model3_data$SUSPECT_HEIGHT -
  mean(model3_data$SUSPECT_HEIGHT)

model3_data$SUSPECT_WEIGHT <- model3_data$SUSPECT_WEIGHT -
  mean(model3_data$SUSPECT_WEIGHT)

model3_data$log_OBSERVED_DURATION <- model3_data$log_OBSERVED_DURATION -
  mean(model3_data$log_OBSERVED_DURATION)

model3_data$log_STOP_DURATION <- model3_data$log_STOP_DURATION -
  mean(model3_data$log_STOP_DURATION)


#Reference categories

model3_data$SUSPECT_SEX <- relevel(model3_data$SUSPECT_SEX, ref = "MALE")
model3_data$SUSPECT_RACE_DESCRIPTION <- relevel(model3_data$SUSPECT_RACE_DESCRIPTION, ref = "BLACK")
model3_data$STOP_WAS_INITIATED <- relevel(model3_data$STOP_WAS_INITIATED, ref = "Based on Self Initiated")
model3_data$SUSPECTED_CRIME_DESCRIPTION <- relevel(model3_data$SUSPECTED_CRIME_DESCRIPTION, ref = "CPW")
model3_data$FRISKED_FLAG <- relevel(model3_data$FRISKED_FLAG, ref = "N")
model3_data$SEARCHED_FLAG <- relevel(model3_data$SEARCHED_FLAG, ref = "N")
model3_data$WEAPON_FOUND_FLAG <- relevel(model3_data$WEAPON_FOUND_FLAG, ref = "N")
model3_data$STOP_LOCATION_BORO_NAME <- relevel(model3_data$STOP_LOCATION_BORO_NAME, ref = "BRONX")

#------------------------
#Temporal variables

#Calendar date -> ordered index for RW1
model3_data$STOP_FRISK_DATE <- as.Date(model3_data$STOP_FRISK_DATE)
date_levels <- sort(unique(model3_data$STOP_FRISK_DATE))
model3_data$date_index <- match(model3_data$STOP_FRISK_DATE, date_levels)

#Time of day -> seconds from midnight -> 30-minute bins, as in model 2
time_chr <- as.character(model3_data$STOP_FRISK_TIME)
time_posix <- strptime(time_chr, format = "%H:%M:%S")

seconds_from_midnight <- time_posix$hour * 3600 +
  time_posix$min * 60 +
  time_posix$sec

model3_data$time_bin <- floor(seconds_from_midnight / (30 * 60)) + 1

#Grouping indices for random effects


#Borough random effect
model3_data$boro_id <- as.integer(model3_data$STOP_LOCATION_BORO_NAME)

#Command-level hierarchical random effect
model3_data$command_id <- as.integer(model3_data$ISSUING_OFFICER_COMMAND_CODE)


#Priors


#Same priors for fixed effects as in previous models
prior.fixed <- list(
  mean.intercept = 0,
  prec.intercept = 0.01,   # variance = 100
  mean = 0,
  prec = 0.04              # variance = 25
)

#PC prior for all latent random effects:

pc_prec <- list(
  prec = list(
    prior = "pc.prec",
    param = c(1, 0.01)
  )
)


#Fit Model 3

m3 <- inla(
  SUSPECT_ARRESTED_FLAG ~
    SUSPECT_REPORTED_AGE +
    SUSPECT_HEIGHT +
    SUSPECT_WEIGHT +
    log_OBSERVED_DURATION +
    log_STOP_DURATION +
    SUSPECT_SEX +
    SUSPECT_RACE_DESCRIPTION +
    STOP_WAS_INITIATED +
    SUSPECTED_CRIME_DESCRIPTION +
    FRISKED_FLAG +
    SEARCHED_FLAG +
    WEAPON_FOUND_FLAG +
    f(date_index, model = "rw1",
      hyper = pc_prec) +
    f(time_bin, model = "rw1", cyclic = TRUE,
      hyper = pc_prec) +
    f(boro_id, model = "iid",
      hyper = pc_prec) +
    f(command_id, model = "iid",
      hyper = pc_prec),
  family = "binomial",
  data = model3_data,
  control.fixed = prior.fixed,
  control.compute = list(dic = TRUE, waic = TRUE, cpo = TRUE),
  control.predictor = list(compute = TRUE)
)


#Output

summary(m3)

#Fixed effects
m3$summary.fixed

#Hyperparameters
m3$summary.hyperpar

#Fit criteria
m3$dic$dic
m3$waic$waic
m3$mlik

#NLSCPO
nlscpo.m3 <- -sum(log(m3$cpo$cpo), na.rm = TRUE)
nlscpo.m3


#Fixed component

coef_table_m3 <- data.frame(
  Coefficient = rownames(m3$summary.fixed),
  Mean = m3$summary.fixed$mean,
  Lower95 = m3$summary.fixed$`0.025quant`,
  Upper95 = m3$summary.fixed$`0.975quant`,
  AbsMean = abs(m3$summary.fixed$mean),
  row.names = NULL
)

coef_table_m3 <- coef_table_m3[order(coef_table_m3$AbsMean, decreasing = TRUE), ]

print(coef_table_m3[1:15, c("Coefficient", "Mean", "Lower95", "Upper95")], row.names = FALSE)

m3$summary.hyperpar
#Extract posterior summary of command random effects
cmd_re <- m3$summary.random$command_id

#Recover command labels using the ID returned by INLA
cmd_labels <- levels(model3_data$ISSUING_OFFICER_COMMAND_CODE)[cmd_re$ID]

#Build plotting data frame
cmd_plot <- data.frame(
  command = cmd_labels,
  mean = cmd_re$mean,
  low = cmd_re$`0.025quant`,
  high = cmd_re$`0.975quant`
)

#Order commands by posterior mean
cmd_plot <- cmd_plot[order(cmd_plot$mean), ]

#----- Posterior summary of SD for command random effect -----#
# we Select a subset of command effects:
# 10 most negative, 10 closest to zero, 10 most positive

n_extreme <- 10
n_middle  <- 10

#already ordered by mean
cmd_plot_ord <- cmd_plot[order(cmd_plot$mean), ]

#most negative
cmd_low <- head(cmd_plot_ord, n_extreme)

#most positive
cmd_high <- tail(cmd_plot_ord, n_extreme)

#closest to zero
cmd_middle <- cmd_plot[order(abs(cmd_plot$mean)), ]
cmd_middle <- head(cmd_middle, n_middle)

#combine and remove duplicates if any
cmd_plot_sub <- rbind(cmd_low, cmd_middle, cmd_high)
cmd_plot_sub <- cmd_plot_sub[!duplicated(cmd_plot_sub$command), ]

#order again by posterior mean
cmd_plot_sub <- cmd_plot_sub[order(cmd_plot_sub$mean), ]
rownames(cmd_plot_sub) <- NULL

# Posterior summary of the SD for the command-level random effect

#marginal posterior for the precision of command_id
cmd_prec_marg <- m3$marginals.hyperpar[["Precision for command_id"]]

#transform precision into sd
cmd_sd_marg <- inla.tmarginal(function(x) 1 / sqrt(x), cmd_prec_marg)

#posterior mean of the SD
cmd_sd_mean <- inla.emarginal(function(x) x, cmd_sd_marg)

#95% credible interval for the SD
cmd_sd_ci <- inla.qmarginal(c(0.025, 0.975), cmd_sd_marg)

#text to display in the plot
sd_text <- paste0(
  "SD(command effect)\n",
  "mean = ", round(cmd_sd_mean, 3), "\n",
  "95% CrI = [", round(cmd_sd_ci[1], 3), ", ", round(cmd_sd_ci[2], 3), "]"
)
png("model3_selected_command_effects.png", width = 1200, height = 900, res = 150)

par(mar = c(4, 10, 4, 2) + 0.1)

plot(
  x = cmd_plot_sub$mean,
  y = 1:nrow(cmd_plot_sub),
  xlim = range(c(cmd_plot_sub$low, cmd_plot_sub$high)),
  pch = 16,
  yaxt = "n",
  ylab = "",
  xlab = "Command effect (log-odds scale)",
  main = "Selected command-level random effects"
)

arrows(
  x0 = cmd_plot_sub$low,
  y0 = 1:nrow(cmd_plot_sub),
  x1 = cmd_plot_sub$high,
  y1 = 1:nrow(cmd_plot_sub),
  angle = 90,
  code = 3,
  length = 0.03
)

abline(v = 0, col = "red", lty = 2)

axis(
  2,
  at = 1:nrow(cmd_plot_sub),
  labels = cmd_plot_sub$command,
  las = 2,
  cex.axis = 0.8
)

usr <- par("usr")

text(
  x = usr[2] - 0.03 * diff(usr[1:2]),
  y = usr[3] + 0.08 * diff(usr[3:4]),
  labels = sd_text,
  adj = c(1, 0),
  cex = 0.9
)

dev.off()


```


#Model comparison

```{r} 

#Evaluation metrics for all 3 models
cat("Model 1 - DIC:", m1$dic$dic, "\n")
cat("Model 2 - DIC:", m2$dic$dic, "\n")
cat("Model 3 - DIC:", m3$dic$dic, "\n\n")

cat("Model 1 - WAIC:", m1$waic$waic, "\n")
cat("Model 2 - WAIC:", m2$waic$waic, "\n")
cat("Model 3 - WAIC:", m3$waic$waic, "\n\n")

cat("Model 1 - NLSCPO:", nlscpo.m1, "\n")
cat("Model 2 - NLSCPO:", nlscpo.m2, "\n")
cat("Model 3 - NLSCPO:", nlscpo.m3, "\n")



# Calibration plots

# function to build calibration table
make_calibration_table <- function(observed, fitted, n_groups = 10) {
  
  calib_data <- data.frame(
    observed = observed,
    fitted = fitted
  )
  
  #quantile-based groups
  breaks_cal <- quantile(
    calib_data$fitted,
    probs = seq(0, 1, length.out = n_groups + 1),
    na.rm = TRUE
  )
  
  calib_data$bin <- cut(
    calib_data$fitted,
    breaks = breaks_cal,
    include.lowest = TRUE,
    duplicates = "drop"
  )
  
  #average observed and fitted within each bin
  calib_summary <- aggregate(
    cbind(observed, fitted) ~ bin,
    data = calib_data,
    FUN = mean
  )
  
  #number of obs in each bin
  bin_counts <- aggregate(
    observed ~ bin,
    data = calib_data,
    FUN = length
  )
  names(bin_counts)[2] <- "n"
  
  calib_summary <- merge(calib_summary, bin_counts, by = "bin")
  
  #approximate 95% interval for observed proportion
  calib_summary$se_obs <- sqrt(
    calib_summary$observed * (1 - calib_summary$observed) / calib_summary$n
  )
  
  calib_summary$low <- pmax(0, calib_summary$observed - 1.96 * calib_summary$se_obs)
  calib_summary$high <- pmin(1, calib_summary$observed + 1.96 * calib_summary$se_obs)
  
  calib_summary
}

#observed outcomes
obs_m1 <- model1_data$SUSPECT_ARRESTED_FLAG
obs_m2 <- model2_data$SUSPECT_ARRESTED_FLAG
obs_m3 <- model3_data$SUSPECT_ARRESTED_FLAG

#fitted probabilities
fitted_m1 <- m1$summary.fitted.values$mean
fitted_m2 <- m2$summary.fitted.values$mean
fitted_m3 <- m3$summary.fitted.values$mean

#calibration tables
calib_m1 <- make_calibration_table(obs_m1, fitted_m1, n_groups = 10)
calib_m2 <- make_calibration_table(obs_m2, fitted_m2, n_groups = 10)
calib_m3 <- make_calibration_table(obs_m3, fitted_m3, n_groups = 10)

#inspect tables
calib_m1
calib_m2
calib_m3

#Calibration plots

png("calibration_plots_m1_m2_m3.png", width = 1800, height = 650, res = 150)

par(mfrow = c(1, 3), mar = c(5, 5, 4, 2) + 0.1)

# Model 1
plot(
  calib_m1$fitted,
  calib_m1$observed,
  ylim = c(0, 1),
  xlim = c(0, 1),
  pch = 16,
  xlab = "Mean predicted probability",
  ylab = "Observed arrest proportion",
  main = "Model 1: calibration plot"
)
arrows(
  x0 = calib_m1$fitted,
  y0 = calib_m1$low,
  x1 = calib_m1$fitted,
  y1 = calib_m1$high,
  angle = 90,
  code = 3,
  length = 0.05
)
abline(0, 1, col = "red", lty = 2)

#Model 2
plot(
  calib_m2$fitted,
  calib_m2$observed,
  ylim = c(0, 1),
  xlim = c(0, 1),
  pch = 16,
  xlab = "Mean predicted probability",
  ylab = "Observed arrest proportion",
  main = "Model 2: calibration plot"
)
arrows(
  x0 = calib_m2$fitted,
  y0 = calib_m2$low,
  x1 = calib_m2$fitted,
  y1 = calib_m2$high,
  angle = 90,
  code = 3,
  length = 0.05
)
abline(0, 1, col = "red", lty = 2)

#Model 3
plot(
  calib_m3$fitted,
  calib_m3$observed,
  ylim = c(0, 1),
  xlim = c(0, 1),
  pch = 16,
  xlab = "Mean predicted probability",
  ylab = "Observed arrest proportion",
  main = "Model 3: calibration plot"
)
arrows(
  x0 = calib_m3$fitted,
  y0 = calib_m3$low,
  x1 = calib_m3$fitted,
  y1 = calib_m3$high,
  angle = 90,
  code = 3,
  length = 0.05
)
abline(0, 1, col = "red", lty = 2)

dev.off()
```
